name: Chilled Pea Soup
recipeIngredient: sour cream and basil for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Shell peas and steam or parboil until tender. Immediately douse in cold water to prevent overcooking, then set aside to drain.
2. If using frozen petite peas (substituting two 10-ounce packages), cook them instead.
3. Trim the base of the watercress stems and discard. Rinse the bunch and pat dry.
4. Melt butter in a large saucepan and sauté onion until transparent. Add potato slices, watercress, and basil.
5. Pour in broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and cook until potatoes are tender about 10 minutes. Add peas.
6. Remove from heat. Puree the mixture in batches until smooth using a food processor.
7. Return the puree to the saucepan and reheat slightly, then season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Chill thoroughly.
9. To serve, add cream, thinning the soup to the desired consistency. Stir in reserved peas. Pour soup into serving bowls and crumble bacon over each. Add a dollop of sour cream and top with a basil leaf.
